narrative: Jindal Stainless is the overall energy benchmark on absolute consumption and physical energy intensity. It reported the lowest total energy at 30.28 million GJ and the best physical energy intensity at 19.15 GJ/tcs, reflecting a smaller stainless-steel boundary, EAF/recycling advantages and a less energy-heavy footprint than integrated BF-BOF peers. JSW was stronger than Tata and SAIL on absolute total energy and physical intensity, while Tata had the lowest energy per rupee of turnover in the standardised table. Tata’s FY2023 comparison must be treated carefully because the table flags Tata as consolidated, including Indian and international operations, whereas other company-years are generally standalone. SAIL’s FY2023 physical intensity was missing and therefore ranked last by rule. On renewable energy, JSW was already ahead at 1.43 million GJ versus Tata’s 220,000 GJ, SAIL’s 127,200 GJ and Jindal’s 121,060 GJ.
